stance: Risk-on but quality-tilted momentum book: ride rising analyst revisions, prune the refuted cyclicals, keep beta from drifting higher.
experience_note: First graded round (last_round_review is null), so nothing to grade yet — these theses are pre-registered. What I am watching into the next review: (1) does the high-ARM sleeve (ONGC, NMDC, LICI, MUTHOOTFIN, COFORGE, SOLARINDS, GUJGASLTD, ENRIN) actually out-earn the names I cut — that tests whether ARM gave real edge this cycle or just noise at IC~0.05; (2) does cutting the weak-revision Adani/cement/auto-ancillary cyclicals reduce my beta toward ~1.0 without giving up active return; (3) whether the Vedanta-demerger exits were right or whether the demerger re-rates after I left. What would change my mind: if the low-ARM names I exited systematically beat the high-ARM names I added over the next 1-3 months, I should down-weight ARM and lean harder on price-momentum/value next round. I deliberately did NOT chase NIRLON/HAWKINS — if they rip on real fundamentals (not the artifact), that is an acceptable miss, not a refutation of avoiding bad data.
